title: The Straw Hats Are Cornered
description: On pages 9–12, this part focuses on the straw hats are cornered, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Straw Hats Are Cornered. The Straw Hats are trapped beneath Rain Dinners as Crocodile prepares to drown them and erase the kingdom’s last hope. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What's the meaning of this, your highness...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I've come to make a sincere apology!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It was I who stole rain from my people!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: As such, in order to put the sordid Dance Powder Incident behind us,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I-is this a joke, your highness...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The port of Namohana shall be destroyed.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Burn down this city, which stands only as a reminder of corrupt injustice!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: That my town's all dried up!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: I'll kill you for what you did to my towns- people!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: No, stop! It's too dangerous for you, little boy!

summary: This part develops the straw hats are cornered and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
